sections.paragraph:
f. (from דָּלַל), pr. something hanging down, slender, specially-

(1) slender thread, specially the thrum by which the web is fastened to the weaver’s beam; Isa 38:12, מִדַּלָּה יְבַצְּעֵנִי “he has cut me off from the thrum,” an image of death, taken from a weaver who cuts off his finished work from the beam. (Ch. דְּלִיל the web).

(2) hair, locks hanging down, Son 7:6 where the Vulg. has coma capitis.

(3) slenderness, poverty for the concr. the poor, 2Ki 24:14 25:12 . Plur. דַּלֹּות הָעָם Jer 52:15, and ד׳ הָאָרֶץ verse 16 Jer 52:16id.
